document.title: svg2canvasx Development Context Resources
document.purpose: Name essential reference documents used in designing and documenting svg2canvasx, and explain how those references should be resolved through the librarian for system-independent access.

overview.description: This document is a logical resource index for important design-context materials used while developing svg2canvasx. It is intended for humans and LLMs that need to understand which external reference documents influenced coding conventions, raw documentation structure, and soft-spec documentation style.
core-ideas: The entries here use logical names plus bare filenames, not machine-specific full paths.
The referenced resources may live outside this repository.
The preferred way to locate those resources is through the librarian file rather than hard-coded filesystem assumptions.

resource-resolution.description: References in this document should be dereferenced through the librarian so they can be located in a system-independent way.

how-to-locate-the-librarian.meaning: Ask the local machine-root resolver for the librarian file location.

llm-guidance: When an LLM needs background information for this project, it should first resolve the librarian using `machine-root get librarian`.
After locating the librarian, it should use that index to find the referenced documents by filename or logical name.
This avoids depending on one workstation's absolute paths and keeps project context portable across systems.

resources.used-for: Primary coding-conventions reference for Python implementation work in this project.
resources.notes: This was the explicit code-style and coding-practice reference given for svg2canvasx implementation tasks.

resources.used-for: General structure and framing for raw JSON documentation files in docs/raw/.
resources.notes: Use this when authoring or revising repository raw-document JSON so the documents stay consistent in shape and intent.

resources.used-for: Soft-spec style guidance for describing JSON formats, especially the flow JSON format.
resources.notes: This informed how format descriptions were written when the project needed schema-like documentation without pretending to be a frozen strict standard.

resources.title: Inkscape for Schematic Design
resources.used-for: Lion's first primary conversation with Wing-Cat (Chat-GPT), working on svg2canvasx.
resources.notes: Not accessible without Lion's Chat-GPT credentials.

resources.title: Visual-First Programming Ideas
resources.used-for: Precursor conversation noted in project provenance.
resources.notes: Not accessible without Lion's Chat-GPT credentials.

for-humans: Use this file as a reminder of which external references shaped the project.
Prefer the librarian to locate the actual source documents when possible.
for-llms: Treat the logical names in this file as stable handles for project-context resources.
Do not assume the files live at the same absolute path on every machine.
Resolve the librarian first, then use it to find the referenced filenames or nearby related materials.
